Copyright (c) 2021 Rémi Morillon

  create      Create a new .subsln SubSolution configuration file

  generate    Generate .sln Visual Studio solution files from .subsln files.

  validate    Validate .subsln files and their associated .sln files are
              up-to-date.

  show        Show the content of .sln or .subsln files.

result.stderr:
subsln 0.6.1+4ea539e2cbff2ad28e55792c85750ef8b1a1e035
Copyright (c) 2021 Rémi Morillon

  -f, --force       Force to overwrite existing files, without asking user.

  -o, --open        Open .sln file with its default application.

  -x, --xsd         Set the XSD schema location. Allow to have auto-completion
                    in your XML editor even if it doesn't recognize the XML
                    namespace.

  --debug           Enable debug mode to show exceptions and callstacks.

  --help            Display this help screen.

  --version         Display version information.

  files (pos. 0)    Required. Paths of the files to create, relative to working
                    directory. If no extension provided, default extension is
                    ".subsln".

result.stderr:
subsln 0.6.1+4ea539e2cbff2ad28e55792c85750ef8b1a1e035
Copyright (c) 2021 Rémi Morillon

  -p, --preview        Preview the command result without creating/updating
                       files.

  -f, --force          Force to apply changes, without asking user.

  -n, --new            Create new solutions from scratch instead of updating
                       existing ones.

  -o, --open           Open generated files with their default application.

  -s, --show           Show representation of generated solutions.

  -d, --detailed       Show more details in solution output.

  -D, --divergent      Show divergent projects contexts in solution output
                       (divergent configuration-platforms, disabled build,
                       disabled deploy).

  -P, --paths          Show project paths in solution output.

  -v, --verbose        Enable verbose log (solution build, project reading, ...)

  --dotnet             Read projects using given dotnet directory path.

  --msbuild            Read projects using given MSBuild directory path.

  --embedded           Read projects using embedded MSBuild DLLs.

  --import-fallback    On missing imports or SDks, read manually
                       Directory.Build.props and use fallback values. Result may
                       differ from reality.

  --debug              Enable debug mode to show exceptions and callstacks.

  --help               Display this help screen.

  --version            Display version information.

  files (pos. 0)       Paths of SubSolution configuration files to generate.
                       Also accept relative file path using glob patterns ("*",
                       "**" and "?"). If not provided, all .subsln files in
                       working directory and its sub-directories will be
                       generated.
